# @poukai-inc/ui ‚Äî design system rules for LLM consumers

This file is the authoritative brand contract for `@poukai-inc/ui`. Hand-authored by `poukai-design`. Takes precedence over inline comments, story descriptions, or any generated output (including `dist/llms.txt`). Read before writing, reviewing, or modifying any component, token, or style in this repository.

Consumed via: `cat node_modules/@poukai-inc/ui/dist/llms-full.txt`

---

## Token semantics

All tokens live in `src/tokens/tokens.css` on `:root`. Owned exclusively by `poukai-design`. Engineers read tokens; they never modify the token file. If an implementation requires a value with no token, work stops until `poukai-design` adds it.

### Color

- `--bg: #fbfbfd` ‚Äî Page background (apple.com canvas). NEVER pure `#FFFFFF`; the off-white value gives elevation headroom above the page and allows clean dark-mode inversion. The only surfaces that should be `--bg` are the literal page canvas and elements that need to feel "on the page" (not elevated above it).
- `--bg-elevated: #ffffff` ‚Äî Front-most elevation layer. Reserved exclusively for popovers, sheets, modals, and dialogs. This is the ONLY surface where pure `#FFFFFF` is permitted ‚Äî it reads as "elevated above the page" precisely because the page is `#FBFBFD`. NEVER use `--bg-elevated` for page background or content sections.
- `--surface: #f5f5f7` ‚Äî Recessed elevation (one step below `--bg`). Used for code blocks, quote backgrounds, card fills (RoleCard). NEVER use as body text. NEVER use as a section divider. `--surface` recedes below the page canvas; `--bg-elevated` rises above it.
- `--surface-section: #f8f8fa` ‚Äî Page-section recessed background. Use for full-width alternating section bands on editorial pages only. Subtler than `--surface` (halfway between `--bg` and `--surface`). NEVER use for inline elements (code blocks, card fills) ‚Äî use `--surface` for those. NEVER stack adjacent to `--surface` on the same page. Maximum 5 alternating bands per page.
- `--fg: #1d1d1f` ‚Äî Primary text, wordmark fill, sigil stroke, and the `primary` button background (inverts: `--fg` fill, `--bg` text). The only color allowed for body paragraph text. NEVER use decoratively as a background wash.
- `--fg-muted: #6e6e73` ‚Äî Secondary text: captions, footer copy, eyebrow labels, lede paragraphs, hiredBy lines, Stat provenance. NEVER use for primary calls to action. NEVER use as a disabled-state substitute.
- `--hairline: #d2d2d7` ‚Äî Dividers, 1px rules, borders. Always applied as a border/border-top/border-bottom, NEVER as a background. Never render thicker than `--hairline-w: 1px`.
- `--accent: #0071e3` ‚Äî The brand‚Äôs single accent. Roles: status dot fill, link underline-on-hover (the accent layer grows over the resting hairline underline), focus rings. NEVER use as a surface fill, body text color, or button background. It is a signal color: interactivity and focus only. `--accent` is NEVER visible in a resting state ‚Äî it appears only on hover (animated underline) and on focus-visible (ring).
- `--accent-glow: rgba(0, 113, 227, 0.18)` ‚Äî Transparent halo for the available status dot and `::selection`. NEVER use as a container fill or hover state. Never stack it.
- `--bg-warm-accent: #c0452c` ‚Äî Editorial warm-accent band background (full-bleed orange). Restraint rules in `meta/brand.md`: at most one warm band per page; never adjacent to another `--bg-warm-accent` band. NEVER use for inline elements or component fills.
- `--fg-on-warm: #fdf5f0` ‚Äî Primary text on warm bands. Warm-tinted near-white. The only text color permitted as primary copy over `--bg-warm-accent`. NEVER use over `--bg`.
- `--fg-on-warm-muted: #f5ddd0` ‚Äî Supporting text on warm bands. Use sparingly; see `meta/brand.md` for the contrast ceiling. NEVER use as primary copy.

**Elevation rhythm.** Four steps, low to high: `--surface (#F5F5F7) < --surface-section (#F8F8FA) < --bg (#FBFBFD) < --bg-elevated (#FFFFFF)`. Use `--surface` for recessed inline elements (code blocks, quote blocks, card fills). Use `--surface-section` for full-width alternating section bands on editorial pages ‚Äî this is the only tier permitted as a section divider. Use `--bg` for the page canvas and page-level content. Use `--bg-elevated` for overlays that must read as front-most. Never skip steps. Never use `--surface` as a section divider.

**Never pure edges.** `--fg` is never `#000000`; `--bg` is never pure `#FFFFFF`. Both ends of the neutral ramp pull in slightly ‚Äî this gives elevation headroom on the page and ensures the palette inverts cleanly to dark mode without losing its restraint. Treat any spec or hardcoded value that implies a pure edge as a brand-level escalation.

### Typography

- `--font-serif: "Instrument Serif", Georgia, ‚Ä¶` ‚Äî Editorial display face. Used for h1, h2, RoleCard titles, Principle titles, FailureMode titles, Stat numeral. NEVER use for UI chrome.
- `--font-sans: "Geist", -apple-system, ‚Ä¶` ‚Äî Primary UI face. All body copy, navigation, buttons, captions, badges, eyebrows. Self-hosted in src/tokens/fonts/. NEVER substitute another sans-serif.
- `--font-mono: "Geist Mono", ui-monospace, ‚Ä¶` ‚Äî Used for Stat source lines and RoleCard eyebrow labels. Encodes "reference" or "label" semantics.
- `--fs-tagline: clamp(2.25rem, 1.5rem + 3.5vw, 4.25rem)` (36-68px) ‚Äî The h1 display scale. Used exactly once per page for the Hero title.
- `--fs-tagline-intimate: clamp(2rem, 1.25rem + 2.5vw, 3.25rem)` (32-52px) ‚Äî Hero title at `size="intimate"`. Use only inside the Hero molecule. Never use directly on `h1` outside Hero context.
- `--fs-display: clamp(3rem, 1.75rem + 4vw, 5.5rem)` (48-88px) ‚Äî Editorial display rung above `--fs-tagline`. AT MOST ONCE PER PAGE. NOT a heading replacement (the semantic `<h1>` stays on `--fs-tagline` via `<Hero>`). Reserved for editorial display moments where the page opens on a single statement and the Hero pattern is being deliberately replaced ‚Äî not augmented. Sits 8px below `--fs-stat-large` (96px) so numerals stay loudest.
- `--fs-statement: clamp(1.75rem, 1.25rem + 2vw, 2.75rem)` (28-44px) ‚Äî Italic-serif editorial Statement block. Sits between `h2` and `--fs-tagline-intimate`. Used only by the `<Statement>` molecule; not a heading replacement and does not emit any heading element.
- `--fs-pull: clamp(1.25rem, 1rem + 1vw, 1.625rem)` (20-26px) ‚Äî Inline editorial pull-quote accent. Above `--fs-body`, below `--fs-statement`. Used only by the `<Pull>` molecule. Fills the gap between body and Statement; a distinct semantic rung with reuse potential for future inline callout components. No `size` variant ‚Äî one scale carries.
- `--fs-body: clamp(1.0625rem, 1rem + 0.3vw, 1.1875rem)` (17-19px) ‚Äî Primary reading size.
- `--fs-meta: 0.875rem` (14px) ‚Äî Secondary text: captions, nav labels, badge text, hiredBy lines.
- `--fs-micro: 0.75rem` (12px) ‚Äî Uppercase labels only. Always paired with letter-spacing: 0.04em and text-transform: uppercase.
- `--fs-stat: clamp(2.75rem, 2rem + 3vw, 4.5rem)` (44-72px) ‚Äî Stat numeral at size="md".
- `--fs-stat-large: clamp(3.5rem, 2.25rem + 5vw, 6rem)` (56-96px) ‚Äî Stat numeral at size="lg". One per section at most.
- `--tracking-stat: -0.015em` ‚Äî Negative letter-spacing for display numerals in Stat only.
- `--tracking-eyebrow: 0.06em` ‚Äî Canonical letter-spacing for all Eyebrow labels. The single resolved value across the three previously-divergent inline usages (RoleCard: 0.06em, FailureMode: 0.08em, .micro: 0.04em). At `--fs-meta` (14px) = 0.84px per letter ‚Äî enough to communicate uppercase structure without tipping airy. See design spec `meta/design/Eyebrow.md ¬ß3` for rationale.
- `--lh-meta: 1.2` ‚Äî Line-height for meta/eyebrow-scale text. First named line-height token in the system, scoped to the meta register. Matches the value already used on `h2`, `h3`, and `.numeral` throughout the token file. A full `--lh-*` ramp is deferred to a foundations pass.
- `--fs-card-title: clamp(1.5rem, 1.15rem + 1.2vw, 2rem)` (24-32px) ‚Äî Card-title rung. Shared by `<RoleCard>`, `<Principle>`, `<FailureMode>` titles. Always rendered in `--font-serif`, `font-weight: 400`. Do NOT use as a heading-level replacement; the title element semantics still come from the consuming page.

### Spacing (4px base grid)

- `--space-1`: 0.25rem (4px) ‚Äî Tight: icon-to-label gap
- `--space-2`: 0.5rem (8px) ‚Äî Inline gap: eyebrow-to-title
- `--space-3`: 0.75rem (12px) ‚Äî Badge dot-to-text, Stat column gap
- `--space-4`: 1rem (16px) ‚Äî Body paragraph margin, card internal rhythm
- `--space-6`: 1.5rem (24px) ‚Äî Hero status-to-title, nav gap, card padding tier
- `--space-8`: 2rem (32px) ‚Äî Hero title-to-lede, Principle padding
- `--space-10`: 2.5rem (40px) ‚Äî Editorial bump above `--space-8` (h1 desktop bottom margin, Hero CTA desktop top margin, FailureMode top padding)
- `--space-12`: 3rem (48px) ‚Äî Principle desktop padding, main content top
- `--space-16`: 4rem (64px) ‚Äî Large section gaps
- `--space-24`: 6rem (96px) ‚Äî Main content bottom padding
- `--space-32`: 8rem (128px) ‚Äî Maximum editorial breathing room

Other gaps in the scale (--space-5, --space-7, --space-9, --space-11, etc.) do not exist. NEVER use raw pixel values.

### Layout

- `--page-pad: clamp(1.5rem, 2vw + 1rem, 3rem)` ‚Äî Horizontal padding for SiteShell header/footer. The only token that governs the outer page edge.
- `--content-max: 64rem` (1024px) ‚Äî Maximum width of the main content column. NEVER exceed in the main column layout.
- `--hero-max: 38rem` (608px) ‚Äî Maximum width of the Hero text column. Applies at all widths. NEVER override with a wider value.
- `--hero-illustration-max: 25rem` (400px) ‚Äî Maximum width of the Hero illustration column when the `illustration` slot is active. Applies above the 720px breakpoint; the illustration column is hidden below that.

### Motion

- `--easing: cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)` ‚Äî Expo-out for entrance animations.
- `--easing-link: cubic-bezier(0.2, 0, 0, 1)` ‚Äî Link underline grow animation.
- `--dur-press: 80ms` ‚Äî Tactile click/press feedback (button `:active` transform, immediate-response interactions). Deliberately shorter than `--dur-fast` so the press lands without lag.
- `--dur-fast: 180ms` ‚Äî Micro-interactions.
- `--dur-mid: 240ms` ‚Äî Standard transition.
- `--dur-slow: 600ms` ‚Äî Entrance animations.
- `--dur-pulse: 1800ms` ‚Äî `<StatusBadge status="available">` halo pulse (intentionally slow + meditative, paired with `ease-out`).

All motion is gated by `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` via a `:root !important` block. This block is the ONLY permitted use of `!important` in the codebase.

### Radii and rules

- `--radius-1: 2px` ‚Äî Focus rings only.
- `--radius-2: 4px` ‚Äî Button corners and the icon well in RoleCard.
- `--radius-3: 8px` ‚Äî RoleCard container.
- `--hairline-w: 1px` ‚Äî Universal border width. NEVER use 2px or more for decorative borders.

### Button height ladder

The `<Button>` height ladder is part of the brand contract. Four named rungs, hand-tuned, not interpolated.

- `--btn-h-sm: 32px` ‚Äî `size="sm"` min-height. Minor inline action.
- `--btn-h-compact: 40px` ‚Äî `size="compact"` min-height. Editorial CTA ‚Äî quieter than the page default. Apple's "Compact" rung.
- `--btn-h-md: 44px` ‚Äî `size="md"` min-height. Page-default CTA. The only rung that passes WCAG 2.5.5 AAA at threshold.
- `--btn-h-lg: 52px` ‚Äî `size="lg"` min-height. Loud CTA.

Padding and font-size stay hand-tuned per size (no separate padding-token scale). Consumed by `src/atoms/Button/Button.module.css`; available to any future primitive (form fields, segmented controls) that needs to align to the same height ladder.

---

## Component recommended usages

### Wordmark
- Min height: 40px. SiteShell default is height={56}.
- Aspect ratio locked at ~4.08:1 (viewBox 0 0 1184 290). Set only `height`; width is auto.
- Do NOT render below height={40}. Do NOT add a second Wordmark inside SiteShell's children. Do NOT use as a watermark. Do NOT set label="" (it is the a11y name).
- Path geometry changes require Arian's approval (ADR-0011).

### StatusBadge
- Maximum 1 instance per page. Encodes consultant/service availability ‚Äî not system status.
- status="available" triggers pulse animation automatically. Do NOT add additional CSS animation.
- prefers-reduced-motion disables the pulse at the CSS level ‚Äî do NOT force-animate via JS or inline styles.
- Children: single short clause, 10 words or fewer.

### Button
- Maximum one variant="primary" per visual section.
- Labels always sentence-case, never ALL CAPS.
- Min heights: size="sm" 32px, size="compact" 40px, size="md" 44px, size="lg" 52px. Default is size="md".
- size="compact" is the editorial CTA ‚Äî quieter than md, louder than sm. WCAG 2.5.5 AAA tap target (44px) requires md or lg; sm and compact are AA tiers.
- Use asChild with an <a> element for link-styled buttons. Do NOT nest a <button> inside Button with asChild.

### Stat
- Requires a caption ‚Äî numeral alone is not self-explanatory.
- value must be a numeral or numeral+unit string. source should be citation only.
- Maximum 4 Stats per section. Maximum 1 size="lg" per section.

### Hero
- One per page. Do NOT nest Hero inside another Hero.
- status slot: StatusBadge or equivalent only (default variant only; not available in no-title variant).
- lede: 1-3 sentences at most.
- align="center": standalone landing contexts only.
- size="display" (default): title uses `--fs-tagline` (36-68px). Default for all product, marketing, and feature pages.
- size="intimate": title uses `--fs-tagline-intimate` (32-52px). Opt-in for low-density doorways (homepages, principle lists) where a quieter register suits the context. All other Hero rhythm (gaps, lede, CTA, color, font family, em accent) is unchanged.
- variant="no-title": renders eyebrow + lede only ‚Äî no heading element of any level. Use only when the page's `<h1>` lives in body content. The molecule does not emit an `<h1>`; the consumer must. Eyebrow is rendered at `--fs-micro` (12px), uppercase, `letter-spacing: 0.04em`, `--fg-muted`. The `title` and `status` props are excluded from this variant's type signature. `align`, `entrance`, and `cta` remain available.
- illustration: optional ReactNode slot. When provided, Hero switches to a two-column layout above 720px (text left, illustration right); the illustration column hides below that breakpoint. Capped at `--hero-illustration-max` (25rem). When `entrance="stagger"` is active, the illustration is the fifth stagger slot (index 4, 600ms delay). If decorative, the consumer must pass `aria-hidden="true"` or use `alt=""` on inner imagery. The Hero molecule does not enforce decorative-vs-meaningful semantics on the slot ‚Äî the consumer owns that decision.

### RoleCard
- Eyebrow format: "Role 01", "Role 02" ‚Äî Title Case "Role", zero-padded two-digit numeral.
- hiredBy footer: names separated by " ¬∑ " (middle dot with spaces).
- Min 2 cards per use; max 6 per grid.

### Principle
- Numeral convention: lowercase Roman numerals with trailing period ("i.", "ii.", "iii.").
- Numeral is aria-hidden. Min 2 Principles per use.

### FailureMode
- index is typed number, auto-formats as zero-padded ("01"). Do NOT pass as string.
- Index span is aria-hidden. Titles should be declarative noun phrases.

### Statement
- Editorial italic-serif block at `--fs-statement` (28-44px), `line-height: 1.2`, `letter-spacing: -0.005em`, `text-wrap: balance`. Not a heading element; emits no `<h1>`‚Äì`<h6>`.
- `statement` prop is required ReactNode, rendered inside a `<p>`. `supporting` is an optional second `<p>` at `--fs-body` in `--fg-muted`.
- `as="p"` (default) renders the root as a `<div>` wrapping the two paragraphs. `as="blockquote"` renders the root as `<blockquote>` ‚Äî use this only when quoting an attributed external source.
- `hairline={true}` adds a decorative 1px top rule plus `--space-12` padding-top. Default `false`.
- Use sparingly: an editorial statement, not a tagline replacement. The page `<h1>` still lives in `<Hero>`.

### Portrait
- Editorial photography primitive. Renders `<picture>` with AVIF > WebP > JPEG fallback chain and srcset at 4 widths derived from the canonical `width`.
- `alt` is required and must be non-empty. Dev-mode throws on empty alt ‚Äî portraits are never decorative (WCAG 1.1.1). Do NOT pass `alt=""`.
- `aspect` (`1:1` | `3:4` | `4:3` | `16:9` | `9:16`) drives the CSS `aspect-ratio` and the computed `height` attribute. Locked at render time to prevent CLS.
- `width` is the canonical source-image width in px ‚Äî drives the srcset divisors (1, 1.5, 2.34375, 3.75) and the `width`/`height` HTML attrs. Pass the full source resolution, not the display size.
- `loading` defaults to `"lazy"`; use `"eager"` for above-the-fold portraits. `fetchPriority` defaults to `"auto"`; pair `loading="eager"` with `fetchPriority="high"` for the LCP image. `sizes` defaults to `"100vw"`. `objectPosition` defaults to `"center"`.
- `src` accepts either a string URL or an object shaped like Astro's `ImageMetadata` (`.src` is extracted). Do NOT pass `import.meta` imports without unwrapping.

### Eyebrow
- Canonical micro-label atom. Uppercase, tracked (`--tracking-eyebrow: 0.06em`), `--font-sans`, `--fs-meta` (14px), weight 500, `--lh-meta` (1.2).
- `variant="muted"` (default) ‚Äî color `--fg-muted`. Standard section/category/role label register.
- `variant="solid"` ‚Äî color `--fg`. Full contrast when the label needs more weight.
- `variant="numbered"` ‚Äî color `--fg-muted` + activates the inline numeral slot (inline-flex, baseline-aligned, `--space-2` gap). Use with `numeral` prop.
- `numeral` prop (string, optional) ‚Äî rendered in a leading `<span>` with `font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums`. e.g. `numeral="FM-03"`, `numeral="01 ¬∑"`.
- `as` prop ‚Äî polymorphic root element, defaults to `<span>`. Valid values: `span`, `p`, `div`, `dt`, `h2`, `h3`, `h4`, `h5`, `h6`, `li`. Use `as="h2"` when heading semantics are required at eyebrow scale.
- `margin: 0` ‚Äî Eyebrow owns no margin. The consuming molecule owns spacing between Eyebrow and its sibling title.
- Resolves the drift between `RoleCard.eyebrow` (mono, 12px, 0.06em), `FailureMode.index` (sans, 14px, 0.08em), and `.micro` utility (sans, 12px, 0.04em). Migration to consuming molecules is a follow-up PR.
- Do NOT use `--accent` on Eyebrow ‚Äî it is a label, not a link or status indicator.
- Do NOT add truncation or overflow clipping ‚Äî the DS does not manage Eyebrow overflow.

### EmailLink
- Canonical `mailto:` affordance atom. Not a button ‚Äî it opens a mail client, it does not commit to an outcome. Choose `<EmailLink>` over `<Button asChild><a href="mailto:‚Ä¶">` when the contact register is "reach out" rather than "commit to action."
- `email` (required string) ‚Äî The email address. Component always computes `href="mailto:${email}"`. Consumers NEVER pass `href` directly ‚Äî it is excluded from the prop interface via `Omit<AnchorHTMLAttributes, 'href'>`.
- `label` (optional string) ‚Äî Visible link text. Defaults to the `email` string when omitted. When provided, `label` is the accessible name; the email is the href target only.
- `icon` (optional ReactNode) ‚Äî Leading icon slot. When present, root shifts to `inline-flex` with `align-items: center` and `gap: --space-2`. When absent, root is a plain inline anchor. Use 14px icons in `--fs-meta` contexts, 16px in `--fs-body` contexts. Pass `aria-hidden="true"` on decorative icons.
- `qualifier` (optional string) ‚Äî Trailing role qualifier rendered as ` (qualifier)` in a muted `<span>` inside the anchor. Pass the bare name/role without parentheses. e.g. `qualifier="Arian"` renders "founder@pouk.ai (Arian)".
- `variant="default"` (default) ‚Äî `color: --fg`, hover ‚Üí `--accent`. Use on `--bg` or `--surface` contexts.
- `variant="muted"` ‚Äî `color: --fg-muted`, hover ‚Üí `--fg`. For footer, caption, secondary-copy contexts. Matches existing `.muted-link` treatment in `SiteShell`.
- Underline inherits the global `a` two-layer underline rule in `tokens.css`. At rest: a 1px `--hairline` underline is always visible (resting discoverability signal). On hover: a 1px `--accent` underline grows left-to-right over the hairline layer via `background-size` transition (`--dur-mid`, `--easing-link`). `<EmailLink>` is a link ‚Äî same affordance as every other navigational anchor. No underline override.
- Focus ring: `outline: 2px solid --accent`, `outline-offset: 4px` (link convention ‚Äî matches global `a:focus-visible`, NOT Button's 2px offset). `border-radius: --radius-1`.
- `:visited` ‚Äî no distinct treatment (brand decision; visited state creates false scent on contact links).
- `:active` ‚Äî no `translateY` (link, not button).
- No margin on root ‚Äî consumer owns spacing.
- Do NOT use `<EmailLink>` for `tel:` or `sms:` protocols ‚Äî those get their own atoms.
- Do NOT compose `<EmailLink>` inside `<Button>` ‚Äî they are sibling affordances with different registers.
- Do NOT pass `href` ‚Äî it is always computed from `email`. The prop is excluded at the TypeScript level.

### Section
- Canonical page-section wrapper molecule. Owns the vertical rhythm around a section's header block (eyebrow + title + lede) and exposes a children slot for section body content.
- No visual surface ‚Äî no background, border, or shadow in default configuration. Structural scaffolding only.
- `as` prop (default `"section"`) ‚Äî polymorphic root element. Valid values: `"section"` | `"article"` | `"aside"` | `"div"`. `"section"` is a region landmark when titled; `"article"` for independently distributable content; `"aside"` for tangential content; `"div"` when a landmark would be noise.
- `eyebrow` (optional `string | ReactNode`) ‚Äî String values are automatically wrapped in `<Eyebrow>` with default props (`variant="muted"`). ReactNode values are rendered as-is. Never wraps a ReactNode in a second Eyebrow. Always routes through the `<Eyebrow>` atom for consistency.
- `title` (optional `ReactNode`) ‚Äî rendered as the element named by `titleAs`. When present, wires `aria-labelledby` on the root for landmark elements.
- `titleAs` (optional `"h1" | "h2" | "h3"`, default `"h2"`) ‚Äî heading level for the title element. Default is `h2` (page subdivisions below a Hero which owns the h1). Pass `"h1"` when Section is at the top of a page without a Hero; `"h3"` for nested subdivision contexts.
- `lede` (optional `ReactNode`) ‚Äî supporting copy below the title. String values are wrapped in `<p className="lede">` (applies `color: var(--fg-muted)` and `max-width: 36rem` via the existing `.lede` class in `tokens.css`). ReactNode values are rendered as-is.
- `size` (`"default"` | `"tight"`, default `"default"`) ‚Äî block padding variant. `"default"` = `--space-16` (64px) top + bottom; `"tight"` = `--space-12` (48px) top + bottom.
- `children` ‚Äî section body content. Section applies no styling to children ‚Äî no max-width, no typography. The body is the consumer's space.
- **Empty header guard**: if `eyebrow`, `title`, and `lede` are all absent, the header wrapper div is not rendered.
- **`aria-labelledby` wiring**: when `title` is present and `as` is a landmark element (`"section"` / `"article"` / `"aside"`), the title element receives an auto-generated `id` (via `useId()`) and the root receives `aria-labelledby`. Skipped for `as="div"`.
- Header block max-width: `var(--hero-max)` (38rem). Children slot has no max-width.
- Header gaps: eyebrow‚Üítitle `--space-2` (8px); title‚Üílede `--space-4` (16px). Header‚Üíchildren gap: `--space-12` (48px).
- Global `h2` bottom margin is zeroed in Section's CSS scope (`margin: 0` on `.title`) ‚Äî Section's flex layout controls spacing instead.
- No `bleed` prop. No `align` prop. Both excluded from spec ‚Äî structural wrapper, not visual band.
- Do NOT nest Section inside Section. Would double block padding and break vertical rhythm.
- Do NOT use Section as a card, callout, or decorative container. It carries no visual surface.
- Do NOT add `aria-labelledby` manually when a title is provided ‚Äî Section wires it automatically for landmark elements.

### Pull
- Inline editorial pull-quote molecule. Lives inside long-form prose between paragraphs. NOT a page-level molecule (unlike Hero or Statement).
- Root element: `<blockquote>` by default (correct HTML for quoted material). Use `as="aside"` only when Pull is an extracted editorial accent that is not a literal quotation from an external source.
- `variant="serif"` (default) ‚Äî Instrument Serif italic at `--fs-pull` (20‚Äì26px fluid). Use for editorial long-form content, article pages, customer stories.
- `variant="sans"` ‚Äî Geist roman at `--fs-pull`. Use for technical or operator-grade content where Instrument Serif feels mismatched.
- `--fs-pull: clamp(1.25rem, 1rem + 1vw, 1.625rem)` (20‚Äì26px) ‚Äî the only scale. No `size` prop. A `large` variant was explicitly rejected; the page-level editorial scale is `<Statement>`.
- Left rule: `border-inline-start: 3px solid var(--hairline)`. 3px is a brand-locked constant (not `--hairline-w`). Decorative typographic punctuation, not a layout divider.
- `padding-inline-start: var(--space-4)` (16px) ‚Äî distance from rule to body text.
- `margin-block: var(--space-8)` (32px) ‚Äî breathing room above + below. Adjacent `<p>` margin-bottom adds 48px total gap (non-collapsing). This is correct editorial rhythm at --fs-pull scale.
- `max-width: var(--hero-max)` (38rem) ‚Äî matches the article text column. Pull never exceeds prose measure.
- `cite` prop ‚Äî URL mapped to HTML `cite` attribute on `<blockquote>`. Machine-readable only. SILENTLY IGNORED when `as="aside"` (not a valid attribute on aside).
- `attribution` prop (optional ReactNode) ‚Äî visible trailing attribution line. Renders as `<footer>` inside `<blockquote>` (per HTML5 spec; scoped, not contentinfo landmark). Renders as `<p>` inside `<aside>`. Always `--fs-meta`, `--fg-muted`, italic regardless of variant.
- No decorative `"` quote glyph ‚Äî the left rule is the typographic punctuation.
- Do NOT place Pull inside a `<Hero>`. Do NOT stack Pull with `<Statement>` on the same surface. Do NOT use Pull for person identity attribution ‚Äî use `<Quote>` (once shipped).
- Multiple Pulls per page are permitted. More than 3 is an editorial judgment, not a DS constraint.
- Screen reader: `<blockquote>` is announced as a quotation; `<aside>` adds a complementary landmark ‚Äî avoid `as="aside"` in articles with multiple Pulls (landmark noise).

### LinkCard
- Canonical interactive card primitive. The entire card surface is a single click target (`<a>` root). Use for navigational index pages (`/work`, `/posts`, `/case-studies`) where each grid item routes to a destination.
- Root element: `<a>` by default. When `asChild={true}`, root becomes a Radix `Slot` ‚Äî pass a framework router `<Link>` as the child. Identical to the `Button` `asChild` pattern.
- `href` is required when `asChild` is false. A runtime `console.error` fires in development if `href` is absent and `asChild` is false.
- Do NOT pass both `href` and `asChild={true}`. When `asChild` is true the child element owns the href.
- `variant="default"` (default) ‚Äî `--surface` background, `1px solid --hairline` border, `--radius-3` (8px) corners, `--space-8` (32px) inset padding all sides. Use in grid contexts.
- `variant="quiet"` ‚Äî `--bg` background (no fill), hairline rule top only (`border-top: 1px solid --hairline`), no radius, padding-block `--space-4`, padding-inline `0`. Use in dense vertical list contexts.
- Hover: `border-color` shifts to `--accent`. Transition: `border-color var(--dur-mid) var(--easing-link)`. For quiet variant, `border-top-color` shifts.
- `:focus-visible`: `outline: 2px solid var(--accent); outline-offset: 4px`. Default variant adds `border-radius: --radius-3` on the outline to match card shape. Quiet variant: `border-radius: 0`.
- `:active`: `transform: translateY(1px)` at `--dur-press` (80ms). Both variants. Tactile press response matching `<Button>`.
- `eyebrow` (optional `string | ReactNode`) ‚Äî String ‚Üí auto-wrapped in `<Eyebrow variant="muted">`. ReactNode ‚Üí pass-through (consumer owns configuration). Same discriminant as `<Section>`: `typeof eyebrow === 'string'`.
- `title` (required `ReactNode`) ‚Äî primary content and accessible name anchor. Renders as `titleAs` element. Typography: `--fs-card-title` (24‚Äì32px fluid), `--font-sans`, weight 500, `line-height: 1.3`, `letter-spacing: -0.005em`. Margin zeroed inside LinkCard flex scope (overrides global h3 margin).
- `titleAs` (`"h2" | "h3" | "h4"`, default `"h3"`) ‚Äî heading element. Default `h3` is correct when page has `<h1>` (Hero) and `<h2>` (Section above the grid). Pass `"h2"` when no preceding h2 exists.
- `body` (optional `ReactNode`) ‚Äî string ‚Üí wrapped in `<p>`; ReactNode ‚Üí pass-through. `--fs-body`, `--fg-muted`, `line-height: 1.55`.
- `footer` (optional `ReactNode`) ‚Äî pushed to card bottom via flex spacer (`flex: 1`). Defaults to `--fg-muted` color, `--fs-meta` font size. ReactNode pass-through; consumer content can override.
- `icon` (optional `ReactNode`) ‚Äî absolutely positioned at `top: --space-8; right: --space-8` within card padding box. `pointer-events: none`. Consumer-supplied. Conventional: `<ArrowUpRight size={16} />` from `lucide-react` for external links.
- `external` ‚Äî when true: adds `target="_blank"`, `rel="noopener noreferrer"`, and appends a visually-hidden `<span>(opens in new tab)</span>` for screen readers. Does NOT auto-inject an icon ‚Äî pass via `icon` slot.
- Global `<a>` underline animation (`background-image` grow) is suppressed on the card root via `background-image: none; padding-bottom: 0`. The card's sole hover signal is the border-color shift.
- **No nested interactive elements.** A `<button>` or `<a>` inside a LinkCard is a WCAG failure (nested interactivity). Footer tags and inline labels must be non-interactive (`<span>`, plain text). This is documented as a hard constraint, not a component-level enforcement.
- Accessible name is derived from text content ‚Äî eyebrow + title + body all contribute. Body copy should be meaningful, not boilerplate ("Click to learn more" produces a poor accessible name).
- In a CSS Grid context: set `align-items: stretch` on the grid container and `height: 100%` on the LinkCard root so all cards in a row share the same height. The footer flex spacer then aligns footers to the same baseline.
- Do NOT mix `variant="default"` and `variant="quiet"` in the same grid ‚Äî incoherent visual rhythm.
- Do NOT put `<Button>` or any interactive element inside a LinkCard.
- Do NOT use LinkCard for non-navigational content. Use `RoleCard` (structural, no click target) for role descriptions.

### TeamCard
- Canonical person tile molecule. Renders a single team member as a brand object: portrait + name + role + optional bio + optional contact affordance. Operator-grade register ‚Äî practitioner credential, not a social media card.
- Root element: `<article>` by default (correct HTML for a self-contained, independently distributable content item). Polymorphic via `as` prop: `"article"` | `"section"` | `"div"`.
- `aria-labelledby` is wired automatically from the root landmark element to the name heading's `id` (via `useId()`). Skipped when `as="div"` ‚Äî a `<div>` carries no landmark role.
- `portrait` (required `ReactNode`) ‚Äî pass a fully-configured `<Portrait>` instance. TeamCard does NOT accept `portraitSrc` ‚Äî Portrait's API is authoritative for `src`, `alt`, `aspect`, `width`, and `sizes`. Never pass a raw `<img>`.
- `name` (required `ReactNode`) ‚Äî person's name. Rendered as the element specified by `nameAs`. Default `nameAs="h3"` is correct inside a `<Section>` (h2) on a page with a Hero (h1). Override to `"h2"` for standalone person pages; `"h4"` for deeply nested contexts.
- `role` (required `ReactNode`) ‚Äî short role/title descriptor. Rendered as `<p>` at `--fs-meta` (14px), `color: var(--fg-muted)`. No uppercase treatment ‚Äî role is read at prose speed.
- `bio` (optional `ReactNode`) ‚Äî supporting prose paragraph. Rendered as `<p>` at `--fs-body`, `color: var(--fg)`, `line-height: 1.55`. When absent, no empty element is emitted.
- `contact` (optional `ReactNode`) ‚Äî any contact affordance. Standard usage: `<EmailLink email="‚Ä¶" variant="muted" />`. Slot is untyped ‚Äî LinkedIn links, GitHub handles, etc. work without spec changes. TeamCard provides no interaction states; the inner component owns them.
- `eyebrow` (optional `string | ReactNode`) ‚Äî string values are automatically wrapped in `<Eyebrow variant="muted">`. ReactNode values are rendered as-is (escape hatch). Never double-wraps a ReactNode. In grids, prefer placing the eyebrow in the Section header rather than repeating it on every card.
- `layout` (`"stacked"` | `"horizontal"`, default `"stacked"`) ‚Äî portrait-text spatial relationship. `"stacked"`: portrait above, text below (grid-context default). `"horizontal"`: portrait left at fixed `5rem` width, text block right with `--space-6` gap; collapses to stacked below 768px.
- **No padding, no border, no background.** TeamCard is a content tile, not a chrome tile. The consumer's grid owns the column gutter. If a surface variant is needed, use `className` override at the consumer layer ‚Äî it is not a DS variant.
- Gap stack (stacked layout): eyebrow‚Üíportrait `--space-3` (12px), portrait‚Üíname `--space-4` (16px), name‚Üírole `--space-2` (8px), role‚Üíbio `--space-4` (16px), bio‚Üícontact `--space-8` (32px), role‚Üícontact (no bio) `--space-8` (32px).
- Contrast: name heading `--fg` on `--bg` = 16.29:1 (AAA). Role `--fg-muted` on `--bg` = 4.91:1 (AA normal at 14px). Bio `--fg` on `--bg` = 16.29:1 (AAA).
- Do NOT pass `portraitSrc` ‚Äî the prop does not exist. Portrait's API is authoritative.
- Do NOT make TeamCard interactive (`href`, `onClick`). It is not a link. Consumer wraps in `<a>` or `<Link>` if needed and owns that interaction pattern.
- Do NOT nest TeamCard inside Hero. Hero is the page h1 moment; heading-level conflict results.
- Do NOT compose TeamCard with RoleCard. They are sibling molecules with distinct semantics (person vs. function).

### FeatureCard
- Canonical structural feature-grid tile. Use for capability, service, or feature grids where items are peers (not a numbered sequence) and none of the tiles are navigational destinations.
- `as` prop (default `"article"`) ‚Äî polymorphic root element. Valid values: `"article"` | `"section"` | `"div"` | `"li"`. Use `"li"` inside a `<ul>` feature list; `"section"` when region landmark semantics are required; `"div"` when landmark semantics would be noise. Consumer is responsible for wrapping `as="li"` FeatureCards in a `<ul>`.
- `icon` (optional ReactNode) ‚Äî decorative icon. FeatureCard wraps it in `<span aria-hidden="true">`. Sets `color: currentColor` on the wrapper so the icon inherits `--fg`. Recommended icon sizes: `size={24}` or `size={32}` from lucide-react. Consumer still passes `aria-hidden="true"` on the icon node for clarity, but the wrapper guarantees screen readers skip it regardless.
- `eyebrow` (optional `string | ReactNode`) ‚Äî String ‚Üí auto-wrapped in `<Eyebrow variant="muted">`. ReactNode ‚Üí rendered as-is (no double-wrap). Same string-convention as Section, LinkCard, and TeamCard.
- `title` (required ReactNode) ‚Äî feature name. Rendered as the element named by `titleAs`. `"h1"` is intentionally excluded ‚Äî a feature tile is never a page heading.
- `titleAs` (optional `"h2" | "h3" | "h4"`, default `"h3"`) ‚Äî heading level. `"h3"` is correct for a grid of tiles under a `<Section>` (h2) on a page with a `<Hero>` (h1).
- `body` (required ReactNode) ‚Äî feature description. String ‚Üí wrapped in `<p style="margin:0">` (suppresses global `p` bottom-margin double-stack inside flex column). ReactNode ‚Üí rendered as-is.
- `footer` (optional ReactNode) ‚Äî rendered inside a wrapper div with default `color: var(--fg-muted)` and `font-size: var(--fs-meta)`. When absent, no element is emitted and no gap is created.
- `variant` (`"default"` | `"bordered"`, default `"default"`) ‚Äî `"default"` is transparent, no border, no radius. `"bordered"` adds `--surface` background, `1px solid --hairline` border, `--radius-3` corners.
- Padding: `--space-6` (24px) on all four sides in both variants ‚Äî single value prevents layout shift when switching variants at runtime.
- Gap stack: icon‚Üíeyebrow/title `--space-6` (24px); eyebrow‚Üítitle `--space-2` (8px); title‚Üíbody `--space-3` (12px); body‚Üífooter `--space-6` (24px).
- `aria-labelledby` wired on `article` and `section` roots to the title's auto-generated id (via `useId()`). Omitted for `div` and `li` ‚Äî neither carries a landmark role.
- No internal flex spacer. Footer alignment in a grid requires composition: `align-items: stretch` on the grid, `height: 100%` on the card root, `margin-top: auto` on the footer wrapper in consumer surface CSS.
- No states, no hover, no interactivity. FeatureCard is structural only. For an interactive tile, use `<LinkCard>`.
- Do NOT mix `default` and `bordered` variants in the same grid ‚Äî incoherent visual rhythm.
- Do NOT use FeatureCard for navigational tiles ‚Äî use `<LinkCard>`.
- Do NOT use FeatureCard for sequenced/numbered items ‚Äî use `<Principle>` or `<FailureMode>`.
- Do NOT pass `titleAs="h1"` ‚Äî a feature tile is never a page heading. The prop type excludes it.
- Do NOT compose FeatureCard with RoleCard in the same grid. RoleCard carries sequencing semantics that conflict with FeatureCard's peer-item register.

### SiteShell
- Do NOT nest SiteShell inside another SiteShell. It is min-height: 100dvh.
- Emits plain <a href> tags ‚Äî framework-router consumers intercept at app level.
- navLabel (default: "Primary") is the aria-label for the nav element.

---

## Anti-patterns

- **Do not override Wordmark colors via style= or CSS.** Set color on a parent instead.
- **Do not use --accent as a surface fill or body text color.** It is a signal color for interactivity and focus only.
- **Do not use --fg as a decorative background fill.** No dark-mode scheme is defined.
- **Do not hardcode hex values, raw pixel sizes, or magic numbers.** Every CSS value resolves to a var(--token) (ADR-0002).
- **Do not render Wordmark below height={40}.** Isotype detail collapses into illegibility.
- **Do not change the Wordmark aspect ratio.** Use only height; width is auto.
- **Do not use the stacked lockup as the default.** The horizontal lockup is canonical; stacked is for square-format surfaces only.
- **Do not nest Hero inside another Hero.** Invalid ARIA structure, duplicate h1, compounded max-width.
- **Do not use :global selectors in .module.css files.** ADR-0002 prohibits this.
- **Do not force-animate StatusBadge under prefers-reduced-motion.** The !important block in tokens.css handles it.
- **Do not import from sub-paths in tests.** Import from the component file directly.
- **Do not import from dist/ in source files.** Import from src/.
- **Do not make prop removals or renames without a major version bump.**
- **Do not ship a component without both a *.test.tsx and *.stories.tsx.**
- **Do not use `--bg-elevated` as the page background.** It is for overlays only. The page is always `--bg`.
- **Do not hardcode `#FFFFFF` for page background or content sections.** `#FFFFFF` is reserved for `--bg-elevated`. Use `--bg` (`#FBFBFD`) for the page.
- **Do not collapse the elevation rhythm.** `--surface`, `--surface-section`, `--bg`, and `--bg-elevated` are four distinct steps. Skipping or reversing them (e.g. using `--bg-elevated` where `--bg` belongs, or using `--surface` as a section divider) is a brand-level error. Use `--surface-section` for section bands; never substitute `--surface` for that role.
- **Do not use `variant="no-title"` without placing an `<h1>` in body content.** The molecule emits no heading element; the consumer is solely responsible. A page with `variant="no-title"` Hero and no `<h1>` anywhere is an accessibility violation (WCAG 2.4.6, axe-core `page-has-heading-one`).

---

## Brand voice

Precise, direct, technically confident. Never hedges, inflates, or reassures. No startup boilerplate ("unlock," "leverage," "empower," "transform").

- Eyebrows: "Role 01", "Role 02" (structural labels, not prose)
- StatusBadge: "Taking conversations for Q3." / "Not taking new work at this time." (availability facts, no exclamation marks)
- Button labels: sentence-case, max 4 words, specific verbs. "Get in touch", "Learn more", not "Click here" or "LET'S CONNECT!"
- Error states (consumer responsibility): declarative, specific, not apologetic. "Email address is required." not "Oops! Something went wrong."

---

## Decision provenance

ADR-0001: Atomic design taxonomy (atoms/molecules/organisms). No fourth layer. Subpath exports are semver-governed.
ADR-0002: CSS Modules; all values via tokens. No hex, raw px, or magic numbers. tokens.css owned by poukai-design.
ADR-0003: Changesets for semver. Every PR needs a changeset. Prop removals = major. Token additions = minor.
ADR-0004: Playwright CT + axe-core. Zero a11y violations. Every component needs *.test.tsx.
ADR-0005: Ladle for showcase. Every component needs *.stories.tsx.
ADR-0006: Scope @poukai-inc/ui (GitHub Packages).
ADR-0007: vite-plugin-lib-inject-css. cssCodeSplit: true required.
ADR-0008: SiteShell Wordmark height=56. Brand floor; do not pass height < 40.
ADR-0009: Horizontal lockup is canonical. Stacked for square-format only.
ADR-0010: Proposal-loop architecture. proposal:approved label is a human gate.
ADR-0011: Wordmark gap 120 SVG units, wordtype 1.8x, isotype 1.25x. ViewBox 0 0 1184 290.
